novel_features.name: Cross-calendar conflicts
novel_features.description: Find overlapping events across all your Outlook calendars in one pass — the double-bookings Outlook's own UI never shows.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a SQL self-join over locally-synced events; no Graph endpoint returns overlap pairs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent needs to know whether a proposed time blocks the user, this is the source of truth across every calendar the user owns.

novel_features.name: Free-time finder with working hours
novel_features.description: Compute N-minute gaps in your working hours over the next K days, honoring all calendars and optional OOF/tentative exclusion.
novel_features.rationale: getSchedule is degraded on personal Microsoft accounts; local merged-busy-intervals is the only reliable path.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the agent needs to propose a meeting time, this gives a deterministic answer that respects the user's actual working hours.

novel_features.name: Weekly review (change set)
novel_features.description: Diff against the last delta-sync snapshot: what was added, rescheduled, cancelled, or had its RSVP change.
novel_features.rationale: The events/delta endpoint provides the cursor; the diff UX requires retaining the prior snapshot in SQLite.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets the agent answer "what changed since I last looked?" without scanning the whole week.

novel_features.name: Pending invites
novel_features.description: List events whose RSVP is still pending and whose start time is in the future, ordered by start.
novel_features.rationale: Local filter on responseStatus + start_utc that the API does not expose as a single endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agent task: "what invites do I still need to answer?" — answered in one query.

novel_features.name: Recurring drift
novel_features.description: For each recurring-series master, list instances whose start/end/subject/location diverged from the master pattern.
novel_features.rationale: Requires comparing instance rows to a projected master pattern locally; no Graph endpoint emits this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catches the silent organizer-side reschedules that cause people to join Teams calls at the wrong hour.

novel_features.name: Meeting prep dossier
novel_features.description: For upcoming events in the next N hours, return a dossier: subject, location, attendee emails, organizer, body excerpt, attachments list, recurrence/online-meeting flags.
novel_features.rationale: Pre-joined local data assembled in one shot; agent-shaped JSON.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Single tool call that gives an agent everything needed to brief the user on what's coming up.

novel_features.name: Attendee co-occurrence
novel_features.description: How often have I met with this person, and when did I see them last? Counts and recent N events from local store.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-event aggregation by attendee; no Graph endpoint aggregates per-attendee history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agent task: "how often do I meet with X?" without reading the whole calendar.

novel_features.name: Time-zone audit
novel_features.description: Surface events whose start time-zone differs from the calendar's default or from their own end-time TZ — likely-broken displays on other devices.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local filter; surfaces a real Outlook gotcha that the API's own output makes hard to spot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agent task: "are any of my events about to render at the wrong hour for someone?" — yes/no with the offenders.

narrative.headline: The first Outlook calendar CLI built for AI agents on personal Microsoft 365 accounts — with offline conflict detection, free-time math, and recurring-drift insights no Graph endpoint can give you.
narrative.value_prop: Drives your personal Microsoft 365 calendar from scripts and agents. OAuth 2.0 device-code flow against the /common tenant, so personal MSAs work alongside work accounts. A local SQLite store synced through events/delta unlocks `conflicts`, `freetime`, `review`, `pending`, `recurring-drift`, and `prep` — workflows no other Outlook CLI exposes because they require persisted state.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authentication uses OAuth 2.0 device-code flow against `https://login.microsoftonline.com/common`. Run `outlook-calendar-pp-cli auth login --device-code` once; visit the displayed URL on any device, enter the code, and you're done. Refresh tokens are cached at `~/.config/outlook-calendar-pp-cli/token.json` (mode 0600). Your Azure app registration must be a public client with supported account types set to **Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts** (`AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount`); scopes `Calendars.ReadWrite User.Read offline_access`. Personal Microsoft accounts (Outlook.com, Hotmail, Live, MSA) are first-class and tested.

quickstart.comment: One-time interactive login; subsequent commands are non-interactive and refresh-token driven.
Pull events into the local SQLite store via /me/events/delta. Run periodically to keep transcendence features fresh.
Today's week as JSON, ready for an agent.
Find any double-bookings across every calendar you own.
Hour-long open slots in working hours over the next week.
Agent-shaped dossier for the next four hours of meetings.

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login fails with AADSTS50059 'No tenant-identifying information found'
troubleshoots.fix: Confirm your Azure app's Supported account types is `AzureADandPersonalMicrosoftAccount`. Re-register if it was set to single-tenant.

troubleshoots.symptom: auth login fails with AADSTS70016 polling timeout
troubleshoots.fix: Re-run `auth login --device-code`; the polling window is ~15 minutes.
Run `outlook-calendar-pp-cli auth refresh` to rotate the access token, or just re-run the failing command (the client refreshes automatically).

troubleshoots.symptom: getSchedule returns degraded data on a personal MSA
troubleshoots.fix: Use `freetime` (offline, your own calendars only) instead of `availability schedule` — getSchedule is documented as degraded on consumer accounts.

troubleshoots.symptom: Events show in the wrong time zone in --json output
troubleshoots.fix: Pass `--prefer-tz America/New_York` (or another IANA zone). The CLI sets the `Prefer: outlook.timezone="<tz>"` header so all times return in that zone.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for outlook-calendar-pp-cli when an agent needs to read or write a personal Microsoft 365 calendar non-interactively. It is the right choice for daily-brief, schedule-this-meeting, find-me-time, what-changed-this-week, and prep-me-for-my-next-meeting tasks. Prefer it over hitting Microsoft Graph directly when you want offline-shaped queries (conflicts, free-time math, recurring drift) that the Graph endpoints don't expose as single calls.

recipes.title: Daily brief
recipes.explanation: Agent-friendly daily snapshot with all the fields a briefing needs and nothing else.

recipes.title: Find an hour next week
recipes.explanation: Returns gap windows in working hours, OOF excluded — pipe to `jq '.[0]'` for the first opening.

recipes.title: What changed since Monday
recipes.explanation: Diff buckets (added/rescheduled/cancelled/rsvp-changed) that surface organizer-side reschedules.

recipes.title: Pending RSVPs
recipes.explanation: All future events whose RSVP is still pending — narrow output via `--select` so the agent only sees the fields it needs.

recipes.title: Conflict scan with deep `--select`
recipes.explanation: Agents on multi-calendar users (consultant + personal) hit `conflicts` first to know what's actually colliding; dotted `--select` keeps the payload tiny.

trigger_phrases: find me an hour next week
what meetings haven't I responded to
prep me for my next meeting
schedule a meeting on my Outlook calendar
